choices: Alexis, Chazz, and Bastion Have a Meeting
1.content:
"Things like this are really getting old," muttered Jaden.

"Oh shut it. You were practically begging for something like this to happen," retorted Chazz.

"I was not!"

"You were, too!"

It was in the dead of night that Jaden and Chazz bickered as they sprinted through the forest. Their spirit keys were glowing and being tugged. As the guardians of the keys, the two had no choice but to follow. And follow they did until the two them came to a clearing in the forest. In the center of the forest, a masked and caped individual leaned against a giant boulder.

At the sight of them, the masked individual smirked and stood to their full height. "Finally. I was beginning to wonder if a Key Keeper would ever answer my invitation."

"Well, you asked and now we're here," replied Jaden, placing his deck in his Duel Disk. "I don't think I even need to ask, but it's a duel you want, right? If so, Get Your Game On!"

"Hey, wait a minute! Why do you get to Duel him?!" complained Chazz. "You got to duel Nightshroud and Camula. Why not give someone else a turn? Like me, The Chazz."

Jaden scratched the back of his head. "Oh, I mean I guess you could. But I'm kinda the one with the magic pendant. If this Shadow Rider has nasty tricks up his sleeve like Camula did, I'm the best fit for the job!"

Chazz crossed his arms, a scowl on his face. "How come you only make sense when it comes to dueling?"

"It seems the two of you have decided whom my opponent will be," said the Shadow Rider. "As for your question, yes. This will be a Duel. If I win, your Spirit Key will belong to me. If I lose, I will humbly retreat and never again pursue the Spirit Keys or the Sacred Beast cards. What do you say, Key Keeper?"

Jaden smirked. "I say Get Your Game On!"

"Hehe, Very Well."

"DUEL!!!"

Shadow Rider: 4000LP

Jaden: 4000LP


"Since you invited us here, I'll take the first move!" declared Jaden. He drew, arching his arm overdramatically. At the sight of his draw, a huge grin split across his face. "What a draw! I activate the Spell Card Polymerization. This allows me to Fusion Summon a Monster by combining the forces of two or more Monsters on my Field and in my hand. I'll combine Elemental Hero Avian and Elemental Hero Burstinatrix to Summon Elemental Hero Flame Wingman in Attack Position!"

A large winged hero with a fire-breathing dragon-head for its left hand swooped onto the battle field. Jaden's signature monster stared down the Shadow Rider, standing tall and strong. (6/2100/1200).

"Then, to finish things off, I'll throw down a face-down card."

From the sidelines, Chazz whistled. "Only turn one, and Jaden already busted out his strongest monster. At this rate, that Shadow Rider will be toast."

As he made that observation, Chazz heard shuffling and huffing behind him. Tensing, Chazz turned, not sure what he was expecting but more than ready for a duel. Two golden lights were in the distance. It wasn't long before they were close enough that Chazz could distinguish the features of Alexis and Bastion running.

"Oh my, it seems the duel has already started," said Bastion between heavy breaths, hands on his knees.

"Just like Jaden to get straight to the dueling," agreed Alexis, leaning against a tree. "What did we miss, Chazz?"

"Nothing. The duel just started. The Shadow Rider hasn't even taken his first turn yet."

"Not even his first turn, but he's already managed to summon his Elemental Hero Flame Wingman? Impressive," commented Bastion.

Suddenly, the Shadow Rider began to snicker.

"Huh? What's so funny, uh, you Shadow Rider?" asked Jaden, suddenly realizing that the assailant hadn't even given them a name.

"What's funny? Oh, only that you and your friends seem to think that you've already won this duel. But in actuality, I'm in the lead!" With that worrisome declaration, the Shadow Rider drew a card for their turn. "And I'll prove it by activating the effect of Izanagi from my hand! By banishing a Spirit monster from my hand, such as my Amano-Iwato, I can Special Summon Izanagi straight to my field!"

The spiritual form of a large, overarching mountain enveloped the Shadow Rider's field. When it vanished, it left behind a fierce-looking warrior with long flowing white hair, battle armor, and a spear. (6/2200/1000).

"Did that Shadow Rider just say Spirit Monster?!" asked Bastion, alarm written across his face.

Chazz glanced at Bastion. "Yeah, so what? That monster is barely stronger than Jaden's Wingman. Jaden will blast it on his next turn, no problem."

"I hope you're right, otherwise Jaden is in for a very tough time."

"How about less Doomsday conspiracies and more cheering?" said Alexis, narrowing her eyes at Bastion, who always seemed to think something was wrong.

"Next, since I have yet to Normal Summon, I'll do so right now! I summon Inaba White Rabbit in Attack Position!" The spirit of a white rabbit hopped onto the field. (3/700/500). "And now that my field is prepared, Izanagi attacks your Elemental Hero Flame Wingman!"

"Oh no! If this attack connects, Jaden will lose his Flame Wingman! If that happens, he'll be at a huge disadvantage!" yelled Bastion.

"Geez, have a little faith, why don't you," said Jaden. "Because that attack won't be connecting when I play my face-down, Hero Barrier! This Trap Card allows me to negate an attack when I control an Elemental Hero monster. And since I do, your attack is over!"

The Shadow Rider growled at the counter. "How irritating! Nonetheless, I still have my Inaba White Rabbit. And while its attack points are lower than your monster's, it doesn't matter because Inaba can attack directly! Go, give this Key Keeper a taste of despair!"

The rabbit shot passed Jaden's defensive line, and a single, long white tooth dug into Jaden's shoulder.

"Aargh!" cried Jaden, gripping his shoulder, where a dark colored mist was leaving the spot that was attacked. "Dang, that bunny really packs a wallop."

Jaden: 4000LP --> 3300LP

"Yes, that's the point of a Shadow Duel. They're supposed to hurt." The Shadow Rider placed a face-down card before ending their turn.

The duel went back-and-forth between the two duelists. Bastion's fears proved to be correct. The Shadow Rider showed deadly control over the Spirit archetype. Normally, Spirit monsters returned to the hand at the end of the turn they were summoned. However, Izanagi possessed a special power that allowed Spirit Monsters to stay on the field. The Shadow Rider, by protecting the monster with cards like Negate Attack, continuously stopped Jaden's attempts to turn the tide, amassing a large field presence. But Jaden, being Jaden, managed to pull a last minute miracle by combining Elemental Hero Bladedge and Elemental Hero Wildheart. The Fusion Summon, Elemental Hero Wildedge, had the ability to attack all of the Shadow Rider's monsters. Since most of them were very weak, the Battle Damage was enough to end the duel in one fell swoop.

As the dust cleared from Wildedge's attack, a voice rang out. "Curse you, Key Keeper! Curse you! I may be dragged into the Shadow Realm, but I'll use the last of my power to condemn you to live the life of a bottomfeeder!" With that ominous (and cringy) threat, the dust cleared. The Shadow Rider was gone, making him the third one to be defeated. But also...

"Wait a second, where's Jaden!?" said Alexis. Looking around, none of them saw Jaden anywhere. It was like he had vanished...

"Hold on, his duel disk and clothes are on the ground!" said Bastion. The three of them converged on the spot where Jaden's clothes, duel disk, and cards were crumpled on the ground.

"Don't tell me Jaden's walking around somewhere nude," said Chazz, nose crinkling in disgust.

"Um, guys, actually, I'm right here," came a squeaky, distorted version of Jaden's voice. The three of them jumped when a doll-sized Jaden crawled out of his clothes. The boy's face was flushed as he stared up at them, hiding his body with his shirt.

Chazz, Alexis, and Bastion stared down at Jaden with wide eyes.

"What the fuck."
1.title: Down to Size

choices: Bastion: Jaden Finally Gets his Ra Yellow Promotion
Alexis: This Time, Jaden Really IS Invited to the Girls' Dorm
11.content:
Back at the Slifer Red Dorm....

Chazz, Alexis, and Bastion all sat in Jaden's room. Alexis had taken the only chair, which was situated at the communal desk, which only Syrus ever really used. Chazz was leaning against the bedframe, and Bastion stood. All of them had grim expressions. Beside them, Syrus, now wide awake, was holding Jaden, who was covering himself was a handkerchief.

"This is not good," said Bastion.

Chazz snorted. "Thanks for stating the obvious. No duh this is bad. Jaden won the duel, yet he might as well have lost it. There's no way he'll be able to duel now."

"Hold on!" said Jaden, squeaky voice attracting their attention. "I can still duel!"

"And how do you plan to do that?" asked Chazz. "You can't wear a duel disk like that. Heck, I'd be impressed if you could hold more than a single card at a time!"

"I hate to say it, but Chazz is right." Alexis continued, oblivious to Chazz's disheartened expression. "The duels with Nightshroud and Camula had you in bed for weeks. Even if we found a way for you to duel, I don't think it'd be safe for you in that condition."

Jaden crossed his arms, but he didn't argue the point anymore. This was so unfair! He had even won the duel!

"What I'm confused about is why Jaden's pendant didn't protect him from that Shadow Rider's powers," said Bastion. "In reality, we might have gotten lucky that Jaden beat the Shadow Duelist at all. If they had stuck around any longer, they might have been able to render all of us incapable of protecting the Spirit Keys."

There was a brief silence.

"Speaking of the Spirit Keys," said Alexis, breaking the silence. "Jaden is still the holder of a Spirit Key, which means he'll still be a target for the Shadow Riders."

Bastion's eyes widened. "Oh, yes, that's true. I hadn't even thought of that, what a blunder. If Jaden is attacked in this state, he'll stand no chance."

"Hold on! Are you saying we have to worry about protecting our keys and that Slifer Slacker now?!" Chazz groaned. "How are we supposed to do that? Carry him in our pockets?!"

Alexis and Bastion stared meaningfully at Chazz. Chazz slumped. "Grr, this is so stupid. I'll tell you what, it's not going to be me! I already have to hear him and see him most of the day, anyway! So no more!"

"If you're saying no, then that leaves it between me and Bastion," said Alexis.

Chazz balked. "Hold on! I'm not letting that slacker room with you, either, Alexis!"

Alexis glared. "And who made you the boss? I'm perfectly capable of protecting Jaden, too. I don't appreciate you throwing shade on my skills."

"That's not what I meant," said Chazz, lamely, cowed. Face flushed, he retreated from the argument, unable to say the real reason.

"Ahem." Bastion coughed into his fist. "I'd be more than happy to be the one to protect Jaden if that is the best for everyone."

"Uh, hello, guys?"

The three of them turned to see Jaden, staring up at them from Syrus' palms. "Don't I get any say in this? It is my life we're talking about."

Bastion nodded. "Yes, that does make sense. I don't think any of us want you to be unhappy." Alexis nodded in agreement while Chazz scoffed. "Who would you like to stay with?"

Jaden considered this. He had three options.

There was Chazz, one of Jaden's greatest rivals and friends. Jaden was confident that Chazz could beat any Shadow Rider goon that came along. The only issue was that Chazz could be a bit of a grump at times. Also, Jaden wasn't sure if Chazz ever washed that stinky jacket of his. Riding in a pocket like that was straight up gross.

Next there was Bastion. Bastion nearly decimated Jaden's entire deck with a single card, so there was no doubting Bastion's skills. The Ra Yellow Dorm also had some really snazzy food, which would give Jaden plenty of chances to dig in on high quality grub. The only real problem Jaden could see was having him brain spun in circles by all of Bastion's formula talk.

Lastly there was Alexis, who was just as strong as Chazz and Bastion. Jaden didn't really know her too personally like Chazz and Bastion, so maybe it was the perfect chance to get to know her better. Not to mention Jaden has yet to see the inside of the Girls' Dorm, so this could be the perfect chance. He did have to wonder how she would transport him, though, because as far as Jaden could tell, Alexis didn't have any pockets on her skirt.

Jaden put a hand to his chin, humming in thought.

"I know, I'll stay with..."
11.title: Alexis, Chazz, and Bastion Have a Meeting

113.content:
Jaden's curiosity over the inside of the Girls' Blue Dorm was overwhelming. With that in mind, he glanced in Alexis' direction. "If it's alright with you, Alexis, I'd like to stay with you."

Alexis smiled. "Of course, Jaden, you can stay with me."

"Lucky," muttered Syrus, jealous of Jaden's opportunity to get so close to Alexis. Now he wished he was shrunk! Why couldn't Nightshroud have shrunk him and Chumley wayback when? Although, come to think of it, Jaden probably would have been the one to take care of them. Syrus sighed. Even his fantasies fell to the cold reality that was his nonexistent love life.

Jaden just grinned at Syrus. "You bet I am! I can't wait to taste the food there. Ra Yellow had lobster on their menu, so you know the Obelisk Blue Dorm has to have something really good!"

Chazz groaned. He wanted to berate Jaden over thinking about food when he was going to be sleeping in Alexis' bedroom of all places, but he couldn't exactly do that in front of Alexis. She was already cross with him for the perceived slight toward her skills.

Bastion spoke. "Then it's settled. Alexis will protect Jaden until we manage to restore him to normal. In the meantime, I will go wake up Chancellor Sheppard. He should be aware of this, especially since it'll be difficult for Jaden to attend classes normally during this time." If the wrong person were to see Jaden's condition, they could lose his Spirit Key.

"Wait a minute, are you saying I get to skip classes and do no homework?" said Jaden, eyes lighting up. Sure, he couldn't duel. But if it meant no homework and no class, it wasn't a huge deal. Still a big deal, but not huge.

Alexis chuckled as she stepped away from the desk and toward Syrus. "Of course that's what you're worried about, Jaden." She offered her palm, and Syrus gently laid Jaden onto it.

Now that Jaden was in her palm, only covered by Bastion's yellow handkerchief, Alexis' breath caught. He was so small and vulnerable. Given his usual bravado, it was actually really really cute---

"Well, if no one has anything else to say, how about we call it a night?" proposed Bastion.

Alexis jerked, having tuned out. She looked between the three boys, cheeks dusting at the possibility that they noticed her lapse in attention. "Er, yeah. See you tomorrow boys!"

Alexis and Bastion walked through the Academy together, splitting at the path in which he went toward the administrative building while she continued toward the Girls’ Dorm. Alexis could barely keep her attention on where she’s walking. Her eyes kept gravitating back to Jaden, who sat on her cupped palms. Jaden was nude from the waist up, having wrapped the handkerchief around his waist like a towel.

It gave her a really good view of his upper body, and her eyes traced his form. But then Alexis flushed and realized how she was acting and refocused on the walk.

Eventually, Alexis managed to get to her room, sneaking past anyone else who happened to be awake.

Once in the room, Jaden whistled at the size. “Geez, you got all this space to yourself? It’s like all the Slifer Red Dorm rooms put together!”

Outside of sleeping and studying, Alexis didn’t spend much time in her dorm room, so she never really came to appreciate how much space she really had. But when Jaden put it like that, Alexis felt a little spoiled by the academy given he shared a really small space with two others while she had no roommates at all.

Alexis set Jaden down on her dresser and took a seat. “So, now we need to figure out what to do with you,” she said, propping her elbow on the desk and setting her chin on the back of her hand.

Jaden stared up at her. “Uh, what do you mean?”

“Well, I mean, you need clothes, a way to go the bathroom, a way to shower, a place to sleep,” listed Alexis, counting all the things they would need on her fingers. “It’s even more complex since we have to make it so no one sees you.”

Come to think of it, how would Alexis hide Jaden during the day? They didn’t have backpacks, so that was out. Alexis’ school-mandated skirt didn’t have pockets, either. Her Duel Disk wasn’t exactly a safe place to store Jaden, and besides it was full of her cards. Alexis couldn’t just leave Jaden in her dorm room either in case something happened to him.

Jaden scratched the back of his head. “Yeesh, I didn’t even think about all that stuff. I guess I can’t use Bastion’s handkerchief, forever, huh? I don’t really know what to do about all that other stuff, though…”

Pulled from that dilemma, Alexis refocused on the one at hand. The Alexis of tomorrow could figure it out. “Well, I can grab a clean hand towel for you to sleep on. It’s really soft, and at your side it should basically be a mattress and a blanket combined. For showering...if I wake up extra early, we can use the sink in the communal bathroom. Same for using the bathroom. Although, that means you only get to go in the morning and at night,” said Alexis.

“I think I can hold it that long,” mused Jaden. “If I don’t, well, at my size it shouldn’t be too hard to clean it up, right?” he said, jokingly.

Alexis didn’t take the joke well, and she flicked him. Jaden yelped, falling onto his bottom. “I’m not your maid, Jaden. If you start peeing on the carpet like a household pet, I’ll treat you like one, got it?” She wasn’t entirely serious, but then the thought of her calling Jaden’s name and him running toward her, butt wagging, was summoned to the forefront of her mind. Alexis’s cheeks reddened when she realized she didn’t dislike that idea.

“Owowo,” said Jaden, rubbing the spot on his chest where Alexis had struck. That’d be a bruise in the morning, he mused, looking at the red mark left behind. “That was a joke, you know?”

“I know, but I wasn’t joking,” said Alexis, warningly. Jaden gulped.

“Anyway, let’s get to bed.”

Alexis set down a couple of white washcloths, watching Jaden climb into them, burying himself beneath the folds. When Alexis was confident Jaden wouldn’t be peeking out of them, she changed into her nightgown, turned off the lights, and went to bed. It took Alexis awhile to get to sleep, her ears honing in on the barest whispers of Jaden’s snoring.

Alexis couldn’t believe Jaden had fallen asleep so easily while she was all flustered at merely having him in the room. Unconsciously, Alexis puffed out her cheeks at how Jaden didn’t seem at all interested in her as a girl. Not that she wanted him to perv on her, but some genuine interest would be nice.

“Get over it, Alexis.” Alexis turned over, closed her eyes, and eventually fell into dreamland, which happened to contain many tiny Jadens. All of who adored her. Alexis smiled in her sleep.

The next morning….
113.title: Alexis

114.content:
"I know! I'll stay with Syrus!"

"With Syrus?!" repeated Alexis, Bastion, and Chazz with varying levels of surprise, exasperation, and annoyance.

Syrus himself was surprised, adding a "With Me?!" and almost dropping Jaden from sheer surprise.

"Jaden, you can't stay with Syrus. No offense to Syrus, but I don't think he'd be able to defeat a Shadow Rider." Syrus' best showing had been against the Paradox Brothers. And while that was an incredibly impressive feat to add to one's dueling resume, Jaden had put in a lot of work to make that victory possible. By himself against a Shadow Rider, Syrus wouldn't stand a chance.

"None taken," said Syrus, not at all disheartened because he knows it's true. He also doesn't want to duel them to begin with. He remembered watching Dr. Crowler's, Jaden's, and Zane's duels against Nightshroud and Camula. Syrus never wanted to be in that position.

"Now, now, here me out!" said Jaden. "I know you're all worried, and you have a right to be with what's going on. But I really prefer my own bed, ya know?"

Everyone almost fell over at Jaden's explanation.

"You're six inches tall, and the thing you're concerned about is your sleeping arrangement?" asked Bastion, unable to believe what his ears were hearing. "There's recklessness and then there's foolishness, Jaden."

"Hey, if something does happen, Chazz is only a holler away, right?" said Jaden, pointing out that Chazz was basically next door.

"I'm not your servant!" said Chazz, instantly. "I'm not going to be at your beck and call. And besides, what if I'm not around?!"

"There is Professor Banner." Alexis continued. "Come to think of it, he really should be here for this conversation."

Chazz scoffed. "Please. Banner couldn't duel his way out of a litter box."

Syrus, not confident in Jaden's decision but wanting to support his best friend, added. "Um, what if I had Jaden whenever a creepy person comes around? If they see Jaden isn't here, they might move on."

"Or take you hostage," said Alexis, pointing out the flaw in Syrus' argument. Nightshroud had taken Syrus and Chumley to force Jaden to duel.

"Err, yeah, that's true," said Syrus, deflating. "Sorry, Jaden," he said.

"Hold on, that might work," said Bastion, surprising everyone. "While Syrus being taken is a very real possibility, the fact of the matter is that the Shadow Rider would eventually reach out to one of us anyway if Jaden doesn't respond to the challenge. This strategy might be capable of completely hiding one of the Spirit Keys since they won't be able to locate or defeat Jaden in a duel."

"I don't know..." said Alexis, unsure. The logic sounded weak to her. But then again, Bastion was the smartest of them, and if this idea had his confidence, it was likely for good reason.

"How about we test it out?" proposed Bastion.

"Test it out?" asked Chazz, incredulously. "You do realize that if we mess up here, Jaden's a goner, right?"

Syrus gulped. He glanced down at Jaden, who didn't seem worried at all. Maybe this wasn't the best idea, after all...

But as the three Spirit Keepers went back and forth, they came to an agreement that Jaden would stay in his dorm where he would be most comfortable. If something dangerous did happen, they would reevaluate then.

Alexis, Chazz, and Bastion left, Alexis lingering in the door for a moment only to be shooed away by Jaden.

"Just us now," said Syrus, holding his friend, who was still nude beneath the hankerchief. Come to think of it, what would Syrus do for Jaden's clothes? He couldn't knit worth a lick. "Well, us and Chumley." Chumley had somehow slept through the whole thing. Syrus was jealous, really.

"Yup! Now let's get some shut-eye!"

"Okay, but uhhh, where are you going to sleep?" inquired Syrus.

"Where? My bed of course!" said Jaden.

"I don't know about that, Jay. What if something happens to you?"

"What could happen? I'm not falling off if that's what you're worried about. That bed is like a desert to me!"

Syrus sighed. "Jaden. What if Chumley or I don't notice you in the morning when we're getting ready to class? Chumley could end up sitting on you while putting on his shoes or something!" Syrus was likely being overdramatic, but it was true that Chumley didn't even know about Jaden's tiny stature. All it would take is a blanket-covered Jaden colliding with Chumley's butt. It'd be over for Jaden.

Jaden paled. "You have a point."

Jaden and Syrus were stumped. Where could Jaden sleep, then? The desk surface was too hard, and if a Shadow Rider came in, Jaden would be in plainview.

"You could sleep on my pillow?" proposed Syrus.

Jaden thought about it. Lying next to Syrus' gigantic head was disturbing, but since Syrus was on the second bunk, he wouldn't have to wrorry about being sat on by an unaware Chumley. "What if you turn over?"

"Hmm," hummed Syrus. Would his head be heavy enough to crush Jaden? It'd be bad if that was the case because Syrus was a toss and turner. "We can test it, real quick?"

"Test it...?"

Moments later found Jaden laying down on the pillow, still covered for his modesty. Syrus, on his side, slowly lowered his head. "I'll go slow, okay? Tell me if it gets to be too much."

"Sounds good," said Jaden, wondering how they had reached this point.

Syrus slowly eased his head onto Jaden's body. "Are you okay?" he asked, putting only the slightest pressure on Jaden.

Jaden coughed. "Yeah, this much is fine. But geez, Sy, when is the last time you washed your hair?"

"Last week."

"Gross."

Syrus flushed. "You don't exactly shower regularly either, you know."

"Yeah, but you don't have a nose full of my hair," retorted Jaden.

"True. I'll adjust my position a bit?" Syrus moved down so that Jaden was pressed against the side of his skull to his cheek. "Is that better?"

"A bit."

"Okay, I'm going to go more." Syrus laid his head down like he would at night.

"Oof!" groaned Jaden.

Syrus sat back up in alarm. "Are you okay, Jaden?"

Jaden waved off the concern. "Yeah, I'm fine. It actually didn't hurt at all! It's just really uncomfortable."

"At least we know that you should be fine sleeping on my pillow with me," said Syrus. That really was the silver lining. Syrus didn't know what he would do if he had to worry about crushing his friend to death. Actually, Syrus was still concerned. What if Jaden got caught in his hair and he didn't notice until Jaden fell off on the way to class or something? Or what if Syrus was punching in his dream, and he was actually punching in real life, too, and ended up punching Jaden? Syrus shuddered as all the way things could go wrong entered his mind.

He swallowed his fears. "Where do you want to sleep, Jay?"

Jaden sat up on the pillow and thought hard.

There was his bed, which he'd be most comfortable in because it was his own smell and not Syrus' own funky boy smell. He also wouldn't worry about Syrus' head flattening him into a Jaden Yuki pancake. There was also the desk, but really that was out of the question. At this point, it wasn't even an option.

"My bed or your pillow, huh?"

As Jaden thought about which of the two to pick, Syrus began wondering if both of those options were too dangerous. Maybe it'd be safer for Jaden somewhere no one could see him and where he didn't need to worry about being hurt. Like a shoe or an underwear drawer...
114.title: Take a Risk with Syrus

1141.content:
Jaden sat upright on Syrus' pillow, arms crossed and eyes shut in thought. After several long moments of silence, Jaden opened his eyes and cranked his head to look up at Syrus, who stared down at him through bespectacled eyes. "After giving it some thought, I think I'm going to sleep on my own bed."

It was lucky that Syrus was laying on his bed. Otherwise, he would have fallen over from sheer exasperation with Jaden's choice. "Jaden, didn't we just go over this? You could get seriously hurt on your bed if Chumley or I aren't paying proper attention! Do you really want to become a pancake that badly?"

Jaden chuckled, scratching the back of his head. "When you put it that way, my idea does seem kinda dangerous. But hey, I figured if I sleep on my pillow, it should be fine. No one's going to go out of their to sit on my pillow, right?"

Syrus titled his head at that. "Huh, I didn't think about that. That actually is a lot safer than if you were somewhere on the mattress itself. You also wouldn't need to worry about someone rolling over you in their sleep."

"So it's decided!" Jaden stood up, forgetting to hold up the handkerchief and revealing his nude body to the world...well, to Syrus.

"Jaden, keep yourself covered!" said Syrus, flushing. It wasn't like he never saw Jaden in his underwear. They shared a dorm room, after all. But it was one thing see Jaden's boxers and it was another seeing his thing.

"Huh? Oh yeah," said Jaden, unbothered. He picked it up and draped it around himself like a blanket. "I'm really going to need some clothes..."

"Maybe Alexis has some doll clothes or something?" suggested Syrus.

Both boys imagined Alexis playing with dolls and then shook their head in unison. "Yeah, never mind," said Syrus, sweatdropping.

"We can figure that part out in the morning," continued Jaden. "Now, if you don't mind, I'd really like to hit the hay. Something tells me tomorrow is going to be hectic."

"Sure, Jay." Syrus cupped Jaden in his hand, which was a strange experience for both of them. For Syrus, it felt like he was moving a small pet, like a hamster, but then he flushed in guilt and shame at comparing Jaden to a house-pet. They were friends, not owner and pet. For Jaden, Syrus had never seemed so big or strong. Each of Syrus' fingers were like trees, thick and strong. Being carried like this really drove it home how small and vulnerable he was right now. Jaden couldn't say he liked it.

Syrus climbed down from the middle bunk and laid Jaden on his pillow. "Oh wait," said Syrus, eyes widening. "Your pillow is kind of out in the open, isn't it? What if a Shadow Rider comes in and sees you there?"

"Syrus, just pull the covers over the pillow," said Jaden, yawning tiredly. That Shadow Duel and all this other drama had really made him tired. He couldn't wait to drift off into dreamland.

"Oh, yeah, I guess that would do the trick." Syrus pulled the covers over Jaden, hiding him from the world. To be extra safe, Syrus pat down the covers to make the bed. If someone did come in, they'd see a made-bed and assume that Jaden wasn't in the dorm room.

Syrus, also exhausted, climbed back up to his bunk. Tomorrow was going to be a wild ride. "Night, Jay," he murmured, falling asleep. Jaden's own tired reply (which was squeaky from his size and indistinguishable from too far away, echoed in the dark room.

The next morning, all three Slifer Reds were woken up by loud banging on the door. Syrus shot up, staring at the door with wide eyes. "Who is that?" he asked, voice shaking. He glanced at the clock. There was still an hour before class! Was it...a Shadow Rider?

“Who the heck is banging on the door this early in the morning?” complained Chumley from the top bunk.

“It’s me, you Slifer Slackers!” yelled Chazz from the other side, hearing Chumley’s voice.

“Huh, why are you here, Chazz?” yelled back Chumley. “Don’t you know it’s not polite to knock so early?!”

“I’m here for Jaden and Syrus--grr, just open the door already!”

Syrus, the only one willing (or capable in Jaden’s case, whose squeaks reached his ears but not quite the words), climbed out of bed, rubbing at his eyes. Syrus opened the door to see Chazz fully dressed with his arms crossed and a deep scowl.

“What the hell. You’re not even dressed!”

“Why would I be? Class isn’t for another hour…”

Chazz growled. “Are you serious? Did you not pay any attention to our conversation yesterday? We need to go see Chancellor Sheppard to tell him what happened to Jaden. After all, how is Jaden going to do anything without people seeing him and word spreading?”

Admittedly, Syrus tuned out at some point during last night’s excessively long discussion. But he wisely didn’t tell that to Chazz and murmured a promise to get dressed quickly. Closing the door, Syrus rushed to change. Then Syrus went over to the bed, where Jaden had climbed out from underneath the covers.

“Looks like it’s time to do some problem solving,” said Jaden, stepping into Syrus’ open palm.

Syrus nodded. “Seems like it. Although…” he squinted at Jaden, who was only covering his front with the handkerchief. “You really should put something on. That’s Bastion’s handkerchief, right? He probably will want it back.”

Jaden yawned. “I hear you, but there’s not exactly something to wear.”

Syrus looked around the room. It was true that they had nothing traditional for Jaden to wear. Syrus’ eyes rested on one of his black socks strewn across the floor. He snapped, inspiration hitting. “I know! How about a sock?” he asked, picking it up.

Jaden’s nose crinkled. “A sock? Wait, that’s not even a clean one, is it?”

Syrus chuckled sheepishly. “Sorry, I haven’t done my laundry in a while. But hey, it looks like it’s the perfect size if we cut out some arm holes and a head hole!” He said, holding the sock beside Jaden. Actually, it's crazy how Jaden’s height was basically the length of Syrus’ feet. Jaden could lay inside Syrus’ shoe almost perfectly. How crazy…

“Is there nothing else…?” asked Jaden, grossed out at the idea of wearing Syrus’ dirty sock.

“I guess we could staple some tissues together,” mused Syrus. That seemed like way more work, though.

“Uh, Sy, why you talking to yourself?” said Chumley, suddenly, leaning over the top bunk. But then he saw Jaden, and his eyes went round. “Woah! Is that Jaden? What the heck happened to him?”

“Oh, Jaden, well---” Syrus began to explain, but then Chazz began banging on the door again.

“Hurry up, Slifer Slackers! I don’t have all day!”

“Right, everyone’s waiting on us!” yelped Syrus. “I’ll tell you later, Chumley. For now... “ the boy quickly went to the desk, where he set Jaden down. In quick, sloppy movements, he grabbed a pair of scissors and cut some holes into his sock. “Here, Jay.”

“Wait, I don’t want to--aah, Syrus, stop!” Jaden flushed as Syrus pulled the sock over his body. His head poked out of the top of the sock. Jaden spit out some lint. “Syrus, what the heck?”

Syrus scratched the back of his head, sheepish. “Sorry, Jay, but there’s no time. We’ll figure out something else later, kay?” Jaden reluctantly accepted this is how it was for the time being, pushing his arms through the holes.

Syrus picked Jaden back up and left the dorm, where Chazz was waiting.

Chazz took one look at Jaden, who was sitting clasped in Syrus’ palm, dressed in a sock, and burst out laughing. “Hahahah! That’s perfect! I can’t believe you’re dressed in a sock.” He leaned over to get a better look, and his nose was assailed by the smell. Chazz pulled back, nose crinkling, but then he laughed more when he realized Jaden was dressed in a dirty sock.

“Hey, it was all I could wear!” said Jaden, face alight with embarrassment. It wasn’t like he wanted to be wearing Syrus’ sock of all things.

Chazz eventually got over it. “Well, let’s go,” he said, smirking whenever he looked in their direction. It was almost like Chazz was engraving the sight in his memory. Only a few steps into the walk, Chazz stopped. “Oh yeah, you need to hide Jaden, Syrus.”

“Hide him? What do you mean?” asked Syrus, stopping in place.

Chazz rolled his eyes. “If someone sees Jaden, this could turn into a huge problem. So just shove Jaden in your pocket or something.”

“Hey! I’m not an object!” protested Jaden, but Syrus was already contemplating where to put Jaden.

“Jay, do you want to go in my blazer pocket or my pants pocket?”

“Syrus!” said Jaden, indignant.

“C’mon, Jay, pick.” Syrus said, firmly, because he wanted to protect his friend even if it meant doing something Jaden didn’t like. “My blazer pocket is uh, a bit cleaner than my pants, but it’s probably easier to fall out of if I’m not paying attention or I trip. My pants on the other hand you should be safe. Although…”

“Although?” asked Jaden.

Syrus shyly looked at Chazz before leaning in close to Jaden. “Er, there’s an inward hole in the pants. You might end up in my pants.”

Jaden’s face turned green. That was so gross!

Syrus pulled back, embarrassed by the admission.

He waited for Jaden’s decision.

Would Jaden pick the comfort of Syrus’ blazer pocket at the risk of being lost, or would Jaden pick the security of Syrus’ pants pocket at the risk of coming much closer to Syrus then he ever thought he would?
1141.title: Jaden's Bed

info.brief_description: A Shadow Rider's mystical powers leave Jaden Yuki at a small and vulnerable size.
info.description:
Hello!

Welcome to my first Interactive Story!

This is a Shrinking-Focused story revolving around Jaden, who is now the size of a doll. Please note that Jaden is the only character who should ever be tiny. Also, he will not be shrinking more than he already has from the first post!

I'd also like to lay down a couple of ground-rules!

#1: No Low-Effort Posts. They will be removed.
#2: No Character Deaths. Chapters containing death will be removed.

For the moment, those are the only two rules. If I feel more need to be made, I will make them and add them here.
